metadata.name: LOOPERS #18
metadata.description: Generative animated painting made from gestural lines traveling through time. Some are rare.

metadata.name: KooBoh #104
metadata.description:
KooBoh delves into the world of cubes and colors within an isometric grid framework.

Press s in live mode for a PNG download
Press (1-5) to change resolution ( 2000x2800 | 4000x5600 | 6000x8400 | 8000x11200 | 10000x14000 )

metadata.name: Boreal #22
metadata.description:
Aurora 'Boreal' made in hydra.ojack.xyz ( live code )

click on 'Boreal' to play embeed audio, 10sec fade-in, made in vcvrack.com ( modular synth )

metadata.name: Possible Ways #103
metadata.description:
This project is written in javascript without using any other libraries, and it is an endless real-time software animation.

The appearance of each token is initially determined by the hash, but the size and positions of its elements change randomly over time, creating an infinite performance.

While working on this project, I was inspired by the elusiveness of the moment, that cannot be saved or predicted. It can only be experienced.

metadata.name: Missingno #2
metadata.description:
Containment has been breached and Missingno is on the loose. 
This unique beast doesn’t have a fixed shape and its appearance is ever-changing. 
Some call it a bug, very few know what it is truly made of.

Only now with present technology we managed to capture a fraction of its unlimited possible faces. 

Mint for a unique picture of Missingno, yet outdated the very moment it’s taken.


(Open live and click on the image to export)

metadata.name: Brosser #233
metadata.description:
Exploring generative brush strokes to simulate real life paintings. 

- A selection of 19 different palettes
- 4 different drawing styles
- Randomized "color swap" between the primary and secondary colors in each palette

Several other factors determine the output of a piece, including angles, depth, brush length, and brush weight, among others.

```Everything you do, it's been done done done before
Everything you say, yeah you said that yesterday```

change colos on click

metadata.name: Dragons #260
metadata.description:
A grid of many lines forming a dragon crafted on paper.
They all have their own personality... Be sure to name and take care of them.

tw: williamapan

happy birthday, fxhash <3

metadata.name: The Good Corals #27
metadata.description: Generative corals  -   but in a good way

metadata.name: Waves and Ripples #140
metadata.description:
Waves and Ripples is an implementation of a continuous CA, where wave like behaviours are obtained from the influence of neighbour cells over the cell itself. Depeding on the connections config for each cell, different patterns will emerge, creating waves, ripples, or a mix of both. When single loop is on, the calculations and drawings are done at once, which makes for a more chaotic visual.
Best enjoyed in full-screen :) 

Click anywhere to restart

Features : 
  shrinkFactor : 3 types
  connectArray: 16 possibilities,
  extraConnection: 4 types,
  mutualNeighbour: 40% chance,
  singleLoop: 30% chance
  monoChromo:  15% chance

Inspired by Matt Pearson's book.

metadata.name: Transmigration #160
metadata.description:
Generative visual composition, hybrid techniques, interactive, textures source created with GAN, assist by AI, eclectic, colors, transform in autonomous system, glitch, made with code, and modular patch.

* Interactive feature :
- place your mouse to visual screen,
- left click to glitch it or see other variations, and
- right click for reset or back to original composition.

The music was produced by @GrooveIury and the code by @nt_worm.

Korg Synth, VolcaSample, VolcaBass and Minilogue were used
through Tascam Porta07 (tape recorder).

Developed using Hydra and Web Audio Api.

Click to get started.

Your machine's performance may affect the reproduction of this work - optimized for Chrome on Desktop.

4 of 5 in CENTURY-XXX, Series 1

metadata.name: IПƬӨ ƬΉΣ VӨID #7
metadata.description:
Na rotina amassada, minha mente esgotada produz esse pequeno vazante de cores no seu claustro — É por onde escoa o resto de minha essência.

A psicodelia deve ser exercitada, mas quando em prol da labuta, torna-se mais uma obsessão do homem por aquilo nada psicodélico — a posse.

Suspire, aceite a simplicidade do subjetivo e desfrute do seu momento de calma.

The music was produced by @GrooveIury and the code by @nt_worm.

Korg Synth, VolcaSample, VolcaBass and Minilogue were used
through Tascam Porta07 (tape recorder).

Developed using Hydra and Web Audio Api.

Click to get started.

Your machine's performance may affect the reproduction of this work - optimized for Chrome on Desktop.

metadata.name: Returns #420
metadata.description:
Returns is an offline-first PWA and an interactive SVG. It combines the joy of interaction, digital color and minimalist sculpture with the potential of a long running, continuously unique visual. Based on “Shipping” from 2016, it is inspired by Anne Truitt, Ugo Rondinone, Imi Knoebel, Kelly Ellsworth and many others.

A subdivision slices the viewport influenced by width, height and depth. Corners are cut by replacing them with two new points, pulled towards each other along the edges. Shapes are “rounded" by repeating this step, keeping the new points close together. As colors change, so do the relations between shapes, even if geometry changes at a slower pace. New compositions are created on click or after a timeout. This progress over time allows for infinite iterations within the space of each token. It is the promise of a seeded starting point, plus the potential for unlimited new and unique iterations and states over time.

metadata.name: The Differential #64
metadata.description:
Noise forms along a crystal edge; points delineate the surface in a thousand clustered moments, each one its polar opposite, joined and viewed from a distance by separate, sylph-like fragments of an instant's slow tick.

At the event horizon, the escape velocity is equal to the speed of light. Since general relativity states that nothing can travel faster than the speed of light, nothing inside the event horizon can ever cross the boundary and escape beyond it, including light.
